tests.expected_output: Shows calendar events and Todoist tasks for today in a concise format, checks yesterday's journal for carry-forward items
Same as above — concise daily orientation with calendar, tasks, and any carry-forward
Triggers the skill and shows today's overview. Should be short and scannable, not a lengthy report

Loads full context: reads Areas of Focus, active Goals, active Projects, today's Todoist tasks and next 3 days, today's calendar, recent journal entries (5-7 days). Ends with 'Context loaded. What would you like to work on?'
Same as above — complete picture of current state with priorities, projects, goals, tasks, and recent themes
Triggers context skill. Synthesises vault data into current priorities and open questions rather than listing everything

Runs full end-of-day ritual: reads today's journal entries, extracts tasks/commitments, checks email, presents findings for approval, files items to Todoist only with approval, then plans tomorrow with 2+8 method
Triggers the close-day skill. Reads tomorrow's calendar and Todoist tasks, recommends 2 must-dos and 8 should-dos with reasoning, waits for user approval before making any changes
Same as 'close day' — triggers full ritual. Should be collaborative not monologue, approval-gated at each step

Creates a weekly review note via obsidian-cli, guides through inbox clearing, calendar review, project review, incomplete tasks, and reflection sections. All Todoist changes require approval. Reflection is user-written with AI prompting.
Same as weekly review — creates note, collaborative review process, 2+8-style prioritisation approach for the week
Triggers review-weekly skill. Should create a note first, then walk through each review step, waiting for user input at each stage

Reads all active goals, gathers evidence (projects, Todoist tasks, journal mentions), presents each with Progressing/Stalled/Drifting/New assessment, prompts user for 3 questions per goal. Offers to add next actions to Todoist with approval.
Same as goal review — reads active goals and presents evidence-based assessment for each, collaborative not monologue
Triggers goal-review skill. If many goals, asks whether to review all or focus on specific areas before diving in

Reads active goals/projects/areas, gathers actual behaviour from Todoist completed tasks, calendar, and journal, presents alignment table with 1-10 scores, avoidance patterns with evidence, and one recommendation per drift item
Triggers drift skill. Focuses on alignment between stated goals and actual actions over past 30 days
Triggers drift skill. Should surface avoidance patterns with evidence (task recurrence, journal mentions, low completion rates) and classify as active vs passive avoidance

Creates monthly review note via obsidian-cli, runs weekly review steps scoped to the month (inbox, month calendar, incomplete tasks, upcoming tasks), suggests goal-review, audits ALL projects including on-hold, optionally checks Areas of Focus, appends reflection sections. All Todoist changes approval-gated.
Same as monthly review — creates note, full review process, goal-review suggestion, project audit
Triggers review-monthly skill. Should create the note first, then work through each step collaboratively waiting for user input

Creates yearly review note via obsidian-cli, reads all monthly reviews from the year and surfaces recurring themes, suggests goal-review, reviews Areas of Focus with evidence, guides forward-looking reflection on goals and projects. Collaborative, reflection is user-written.
Same as yearly review — creates note, reads monthly reviews, surfaces patterns, forward-looking direction setting
Triggers review-yearly skill. Creates note first, then works through phases: year synthesis, goal-review suggestion, areas check, and forward planning

Reads past 14 days of journal entries, identifies graduation candidates by explicit and implicit signals, cross-references vault via qmd, presents ranked table, creates/enriches notes with approval, updates source journal entries with wikilinks
Uses argument to set date range to last month. Same process as above but with broader scope
Triggers graduate skill with default 14-day range. Presents ranked table of candidates with recommendations (Create/Enrich/Skip) — stops before executing, waits for user selection

Scans vault for orphan notes and unresolved links, reports scope (N notes, M orphans, K unresolved), asks whether to proceed with full analysis or focus. Presents High/Medium/Low recommendations with specific edit locations. Applies approved connections in semantically appropriate locations.
Focuses analysis on the career cluster. Finds orphaned career-related notes and missing connections within that topic. Same recommendation format but scoped.
Triggers backlinks skill. Reports scope first, then discovers connections, presents prioritised recommendations before executing anything

Discovers synonyms for 'focus', searches vault broadly, follows backlinks, builds chronological timeline, identifies arc pattern (linear/pivot/convergence/etc), surfaces open tensions. Presents as narrative with citations.

tests.prompt: how has my thinking on productivity evolved
tests.expected_output: Triggers trace skill with topic 'productivity'. Builds timeline from first appearance to current state, identifies inflection points with triggers.
No argument provided — skill asks 'What idea or topic would you like to trace?' before proceeding

Runs all 4 detection methods (structural, thematic, behavioral, convergence) on past 90 days of journals + evergreen notes. Applies fabrication check on each finding. Presents emergences with confidence levels, evidence, and what-to-do suggestions. Ends with meta-emergence synthesis if applicable.
Triggers emerge skill. Focuses on surfacing implicit patterns rather than stated ideas. Should pass fabrication check on all findings.
Focuses detection on career domain. Finds emerging patterns in career-related notes, journal entries, and goals that haven't been explicitly stated.
